title: How trace geometry sets characteristic impedance
description: Characteristic impedance depends on trace width, dielectric height to the reference plane, and the dielectric constant.
body: Use when reasoning about why a controlled net needs specific geometry. Do not treat impedance as a free property, since it is fixed by the cross-section the fab builds. Actions: relate width and dielectric height to the target impedance, verify against the stackup, and record the sensitivity to manufacturing tolerance. Validation signals include a calculated impedance matching the fab report and tolerance analysis staying within spec. Risk boundary: a thinner-than-expected dielectric raises impedance error, so flag any stackup change as an impedance risk before routing.
